name: machine-uart
description: UART (Universal Asynchronous Receiver-Transmitter) serial communication. ESP32 has 3 UART peripherals (UART0=REPL, UART1, UART2).
differences_from_cpython: Does NOT exist in CPython. MicroPython-specific UART interface. UART0 is shared with REPL.

classes.name: UART
classes.signature: machine.UART(id, *, baudrate=115200, bits=8, parity=None, stop=1, tx=None, rx=None, timeout=0, timeout_char=0, invert=0, flow=0)

constructor_params.name: invert
constructor_params.description: Invert signals: 0=normal, UART.INV_TX, UART.INV_RX, or OR'd.

constructor_params.name: flow
constructor_params.description: Hardware flow control: 0=none, UART.RTS, UART.CTS, or UART.RTS|UART.CTS.

methods.name: UART.any

returns.description: Number of bytes available to read.

methods.description: Check how many bytes are in the receive buffer.

methods.name: UART.read

returns.description: Read data. Returns None if no data available.

methods.description: Read up to nbytes. If no data and timeout=0, returns None.

returns.description: Line ending in \n. None if no line available.
True if all data has been transmitted.

methods.name: UART.flush
methods.description: Wait until all data is transmitted.

methods.name: UART.irq
methods.description: Set interrupt handler for UART events. ESP32-specific.

esp32_uart_pins.notes: USB serial / REPL. Avoid reusing unless REPL moved.
Available for user. Often used for GSM/GPS modules.
Available for user. Common choice for external serial.

example:
from machine import UART, Pin

# UART2 on GPIO 17 (TX) and 16 (RX)
uart = UART(2, baudrate=115200, tx=17, rx=16)

# Write data
uart.write(b'AT\r\n')

# Read with polling
import time
while True:
    if uart.any():
        data = uart.read()
        print('Received:', data)
    time.sleep_ms(100)
esp32_notes: ESP32 can remap UART to almost any GPIO via GPIO matrix.
UART0 is usually used by REPL on GPIO1(TX) and GPIO3(RX) — avoid unless REPL moved.
Hardware flow control (RTS/CTS) available via flow parameter.
IRQ support (uart.irq()) is available on ESP32 for non-blocking receive.
UART uses hardware FIFO (128 bytes TX, 128 bytes RX).
